title: Update of Podman to 1.9.2 via Kubic fails on Centos 8 because of dependency issue
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install/Update Podman under Centos 8 via Kubic Repository as described here https://podman.io/getting-started/installation.html

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@eptossjboss05 yum.repos.d]# dnf update podman
Last metadata expiration check: 0:12:05 ago on Tue 19 May 2020 03:40:02 AM EDT.
Error:
 Problem: cannot install the best update candidate for package podman-1.8.2-1.1.el8.x86_64
  - nothing provides containernetworking-plugins >= 0.8.6-1 needed by podman-1.9.2-1.1.el8.x86_64
(try to add '--skip-broken' to skip uninstallable packages or '--nobest' to use not only best candidate packages)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successful update of podman

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@eptossjboss05 yum.repos.d]# podman --version
podman version 1.8.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""

host:

  Distribution:

    version: "8"
  MemFree: 12892454912
  MemTotal: 33559535616
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-60.rc8.module_el8.1.0+237+63e26edc.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 941076480
  SwapTotal: 2147479552
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: eptossjboss05.paradine.local
  kernel: 4.18.0-147.3.1.el8_1.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 2680h 26m 54.89s (Approximately 111.67 days)

  search:
  - registry.redhat.io

    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:

  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Ahh, this is an OBS-build Cent package - @lsm5 PTAL
my bad, seems like some of my changes in the autobuilder job didn't get done correctly for the plugins package. Fixing...
Done. Please close once you verify this works.
@stephan-klein Closing, please reopen if the issue isn't resolved for you.
